voss_identity_correlation_06.title: IDENTITY CORRELATION
voss_identity_correlation_06.description: Match each recovered fragment with the correct identity clue.

memory_name_01.title: MEMORY RECONSTRUCTION
memory_name_01.description: Use the recovered identity clues to reconstruct the missing crew member.
hints: MAINTENANCE LOG WAS SIGNED N. VOSS
RECONSTRUCT THE FULL NAME: NOAH VOSS

events.text: You heard him.
He said you already saw the name.
I keep replaying that sentence.
Not because I want to.
Because my brain keeps poking it like a bruise.
Which means either the archive is hiding it, or one of us walked past it without realizing.
And I hate both options.
A hidden name is bad.
A name you saw and forgot is worse.
That means the damage isn't only in the archive.
No.
I want to say more than no.
I want to give you something useful. A face. A habit. A stupid nickname.
But there is nothing there.
And before you decide that makes me useless, memory is exactly what someone attacked here.
Records can be damaged. Memories can be shaped.
So let's use records. Records lie less emotionally.
Usually.
[ARCHIVE RECONSTRUCTION ACTIVE]

events.title: CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT
events.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
STATUS: ERASED
SURNAME: V---
MEMORY INDEX: DAMAGED
SOURCE CONFLICT: OFFICIAL MANIFEST DENIES ENTRY
events.caption: Partially recovered crew record from corrupted personnel database.
events.message: [CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: V.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I don't remember anyone with a V surname on ECHO.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Grant, Rivera, Park, Owens, Hale, me.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I can say the six names without thinking.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That's what scares me.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If there was a seventh, the silence around them is too clean.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Stop looking.
That file was deleted for a reason.
And no, before Maya says it, not because I'm secretly dramatic.
Although I admit the evidence for that keeps accumulating.
There are names on ECHO that do more than identify people.
They open things.
Sometimes things that should stay closed.
Of course.
You ask me for help, then keep digging the moment I tell you the ground is unstable.
Fine. Dig.
But when the floor answers, don't pretend it was only a floor.
[HALE COOPERATION DEGRADED]
Because names are not just labels in this system.
A name can wake permissions. Doors. Dead processes. Things we shut down for a reason.
People think identity systems are boring.
They are, until one of them starts treating a ghost like authorized personnel.
If this is the name I think it is, you need to be careful.
And yes, I hate how little that helps.

events.title: MAINTENANCE LOG
events.content:
Relay inspection completed.
Signal bleed detected on emergency carrier.
Manual reset recommended.

Signed: N. Voss
events.caption: Recovered relay maintenance entry with partial signature.

events.text: N. Voss.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: No. I would remember that signature.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Maintenance logs passed through Rivera first.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: She caught everything.
She once rejected a report because Park wrote 'weird noise' instead of identifying the noise.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: It was a weird noise.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Noah Voss wasn't supposed to be in that database.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You know that name.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I know the absence of it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That's a very Hale answer.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: It's also the only accurate one.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Someone we didn't bring aboard.
Or someone I was told we didn't bring aboard. That distinction is starting to feel expensive.
There was paperwork.
There is always paperwork before something unforgivable.
Transfer denied. Clearance conflict. Do not assign to ECHO.
I remember the wording because it was too clean.
Clean wording is where institutions hide knives.
Good. Don't let either of us narrate this for you.
Make the records talk to each other.
People defend themselves. Records just contradict each other until something breaks.
Today, that might be the closest thing we have to honesty.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: YOU SAW IT.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: LOOK AGAIN.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: N. VOSS
It wants the name rebuilt.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Or it wants whatever answers to that name.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [IDENTITY CLUE CORRELATION REQUIRED]
[IDENTITY CORRELATION FAILED]
No. V surname, N. Voss signature, already seen.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Put those together, but don't invent more than the records give us.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That's how erased people get erased twice.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [IDENTITY CLUES CONFIRMED]
We have enough pieces.
Crew seven. Surname Voss. First initial N.
That's not a memory.
It's a chalk outline.
Now reconstruct the missing identity. And be careful. Hale wasn't wrong about names waking things.
I don't like this.
Which, I realize, has become my entire personality. But I mean it this time.
If the system asks for a name, give it exactly what the records support. Nothing extra.
Names are keys here.
And some keys turn from the other side.
[RECONSTRUCTION FAILED]
[ARCHIVE CORRUPTION INCREASED]
Wrong reconstruction. The archive didn't like that.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Neither did I.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: N. Voss. Communications specialist. The missing name is not subtle anymore.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [IDENTITY RECONSTRUCTED]

events.title: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
CLEARANCE LEVEL: OMEGA
STATUS: UNKNOWN
TRANSFER STATUS: DENIED
MEMORY INDEX: SUPPRESSED
events.caption: Reconstructed personnel profile from fragmented archive records.
events.message: [NOAH VOSS PROFILE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: There is a silence after the file opens.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: Not signal loss. Not interference.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: Just three people realizing a deleted man has a name again.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: Noah Voss...
That shouldn't be possible. I know I keep saying that. I would love for one thing today to be possible.
Communications specialist.
Of course he was.
The first person erased from the manifest was the one whose job was to make sure people could speak.
I knew the paperwork.
Transfer denied. Clearance sealed. Communications specialist nobody wanted to talk about.
That is not the same as knowing him.
I remember a folder with his name on it.
I remember being told not to ask why it was sealed.
I remember asking anyway.
I do not remember the answer.
That keeps happening, doesn't it?
Because Noah Voss never boarded the station.
His transfer request was denied weeks before launch.
I remember Rivera making a joke about it. Something about communications specialists being terrible at communicating with HR.
She laughed at her own joke.
Rivera almost never did that.
So yes. I remember the denied transfer.
I remember the absence.
Which means the archive isn't only contradicting records.
It's contradicting me.

events.title: TRANSFER RECORD: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
TRANSFER REQUEST: NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
STATUS: DENIED
DATE: 21 DAYS BEFORE LAUNCH
REASON: OMEGA CLEARANCE CONFLICT
FINAL NOTE: DO NOT ASSIGN TO ECHO
events.caption: Recovered transfer record showing Noah Voss was officially denied station assignment.
events.message: [NOAH VOSS TRANSFER RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Denied.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Do not assign to ECHO.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Then why is his name inside our station?
events.speaker: MAYA
UNKNOWN
events.text: SHE IS WRONG.
I was there.
Not on the manifest.
Not in the photos.
Not where they let you remember me.
I was there.
I was the first voice you heard.
Before Hale.
Before Maya.
Before the station learned how to pretend silence was safety.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHY THEY DENIED ME
Manifest fragment gives us a surname shape: V---.
Maintenance signature gives us N. Voss.
Unknown said we already saw it.
So we don't have a full person yet.
We have the outline of someone the archive tried to cut out.
The archive has already given us the shape.
Surname: Voss.
Initial: N.
The reconstructed profile wants the full name.
Noah Voss.
I wish saying it felt less like opening a hatch.

focus: Full Episode 1 / Episode 4 level character pass
Noah Voss reframed as a deleted person, not only a puzzle answer
Expanded Maya memory doubt and emotional reaction
Expanded Hale fear of identity/name systems
Safe fail flows for both Episode 6 puzzles
Added identity and name hint nodes
Final Noah reveal made longer, more personal, and more ominous
